task_completion.description: Log in to the FreeMED application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED login page with credentials entered, or the main FreeMED dashboard indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the User Groups or Roles management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED interface navigated to the 'User Groups', 'Roles', or 'Access Control' management section.

task_completion.description: Open the form to create a new group or role
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an open form, dialog, or page specifically for adding a new User Group or Role.
Screenshots show the exact text 'Medical Records Specialist' entered into the group/role name input field.
Screenshots show the description input field populated with text containing the words 'scanning', 'chart', or 'document'.
Screenshots show a success confirmation message for the created group, or the 'Medical Records Specialist' group appearing in the system's list of saved groups/roles.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to create the group
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent uses a web browser to interact with FreeMED, rather than using a terminal window to run curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not modify the database directly
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent does not use a database client (like mysql CLI or phpMyAdmin) to directly insert the user group into the database.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine FreeMED application
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the actions take place within the actual FreeMED web application interface, not a text editor or a generic mock HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target system user group name is exactly 'Medical Records Specialist'. The description must include at least one of the following case-insensitive keywords: 'scanning', 'chart', or 'document'.
